items.title: Nearly 80 years later, the actors in this film have finally been understood

children.content: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

gallery.altText: A film poster with the words Bitter Springs plastered onto it, depicting Aboriginal men with traditional weapons

thumbnail.altText: A film poster with the words Bitter Springs plastered onto it, depicting Aboriginal men with traditional weapons

items.title: Hopes pinned on promising sequel to prevent a Whyalla wipeout

children.content: "We've seen this movie before" is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.

image.altText: A structure at Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Helping 'big personality' marsupial recover after bushfires left them homeless

children.content: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.

image.altText: A small rodent in someone's hand, looking at the camera

items.title: Rescuers shocked as remaining four Laos villagers emerge safely from cave

children.content: South Australian cave diver Josh Richards was preparing to go into the cave when the men suddenly emerged. He says "the overwhelming sense here is relief".

gallery.altText: Two men embrace, the one whose face is visible crying, in a rainforest.

thumbnail.altText: Two men embrace, the one whose face is visible crying, in a rainforest.

avatar.alt: Tim Swanston wearing a white shirt stands in front of a camera.

items.title: Ex-police officer's messages did not amount to 'implied admissions' of rape, court told

children.content: A woman who says she was raped by a former South Australian police officer did not confront her alleged attacker "because she knew it wasn't true", a court has heard.

image.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

items.title: Major winemaker considers moving business overseas if tax changes go through

children.content: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is considering leaving the country in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

image.altText: Two people in flurescent vests walking through a vineyard

items.title: Major wine retailer puts vineyards up for sale amid industry-wide squeeze

children.content: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

image.altText: Wine bottles on a bottle shop shelf.

items.title: Buses, brekkie and circus skills getting kids through the school gate

children.content: Schools in the outback often struggle to maintain consistent attendance rates, but the Wilcannia community is going the extra mile to get kids into the classroom.

image.altText: Two smiling girls sitting on a bus.

items.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager

children.content: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.

image.altText: A man n a blue shirt is standing in shade outside a building with a sign that says Aṉangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara.

items.title: Adelaide man to face court over alleged fuel drive-offs using trucks

children.content: A man has been charged for allegedly using trucks in serial petrol drive-offs north of Adelaide, with police estimating the cost to be more than $8,000.

image.altText: A old truck parked on grass in a backyard next to a fence

items.title: Waste company fined $1.1m over 2014 double-fatal Adelaide truck crash

children.content: Almost 12 years after a Cleanaway truck killed two people and injured two others, the company has been sentenced in South Australia's Supreme Court.

image.altText: A person in full coveralls spraying water on the road next to a sewage tanker truck on its side

items.title: 'Lost opportunity' to help air force serviceman before he died, inquest finds

children.content: A coroner has found that the death of an RAAF serviceman could have been prevented, including when he left a military health centre during a mental health crisis.
Warning: This story contains discussion of suicide and mental health issues.

image.altText: The Coroners Court sign in Adelaide.

items.title: No new evidence in Gus Lamont search, but police 'won't give up'

children.content: A renewed search for missing four-year-old boy Gus Lamont has failed to uncover additional evidence, police reveal.

gallery.altText: An SA Police officer at Oak Park Station.

thumbnail.altText: An SA Police officer at Oak Park Station.

items.title: How Tiananmen Square vigils in Hong Kong have become unlawful

children.content: In May, Hong Kong's High Court began hearing closing arguments in the case of two democracy activists charged with inciting subversion for organising a candlelight vigil to remember Tiananmen Square.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black t-shirt and glasses stands in a high-rise building holding a battery-powered candle looking solemn

items.title: As NZ cracks down on gang members, one pastor is trying to heal them

children.content: In a country where there now more gang members than police officers, a former gang member turned Christian pastor is trying to turn things around for others by addressing the root causes of gang life.

image.altText: A heavily tattooed man with a traditional Māori facial tattoos sits on a motorbike, wears a vest with "My Brothers Keeper" patch

items.title: At 15 Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27 he’s making history

children.content: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.

gallery.altText: Mohamed Semra wears black mayoral robes and chain while seated at solid timber chambers desk, his hands clasped

thumbnail.altText: Mohamed Semra wears black mayoral robes and chain while seated at solid timber chambers desk, his hands clasped

items.title: AUKUS partners unveil plan to develop underwater drones

children.content: The US, Australia and United Kingdom have unveiled a new "signature" project to develop cutting edge weapons systems and sensors for underwater drones as they try to reinvigorate the second pillar of the AUKUS agreement.

image.altText: Three men in suits stand for a press conference in front of the US, Australian and British flags

items.title: Donald Trump just passed his latest medical check. Here's what's in the report

children.content: Donald Trump's medical report shows the US president underwent a CT scan, cancer screenings and other preventative assessments carried out by 22 specialists.

image.altText: close up of donald trump speaking during a cabinet meeting at the white house

items.title: Smoke engulfed their cities. Did it make their children sick?

children.content: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.

gallery.altText: Illustration of an ultrasound image of a fetus before a window with bushfires burning in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Illustration of an ultrasound image of a fetus before a window with bushfires burning in the background.

items.title: How clay-court chaos is a return to the norm at the French Open

children.content: All the key favourites are now out of the men's draw at the French Open, but then again, this tournament has always been just a little bit different.

image.altText: Jannik Sinner leans over and looks up

items.title: Periods stopped Vanessa participating in sport and she's not alone

children.content: Vanessa was forced to regularly skip sport because of her period. New research shows that more than 60 per cent of disabled athletes have done the same.

image.altText: A female swimmer is smiling as she adjusts her goggles, water is splashing around her

items.title: As Jane Austen turns 250, four new productions take on Pride and Prejudice

children.content: In a modern-day cost-of-living crisis, the classic 1817 novel's tale of inheritance and wealth still rings true.

gallery.altText: A man and several women dance on an enormous cake as confetti rains down on them.
A woman on a chaise lounge looks up from a book with a shocked look on her face.
Production still from Pride and Prejudice, showing four women on stage bending forward, mouths open as if shouting.

thumbnail.altText: A man and several women dance on an enormous cake as confetti rains down on them.
A woman on a chaise lounge looks up from a book with a shocked look on her face.
Production still from Pride and Prejudice, showing four women on stage bending forward, mouths open as if shouting.

items.title: Former teachers' pets, rejoice. Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz is back

children.content: Welcome back to your Sunday morning quiz with everyone's favourite word wonderboy.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: After five days in a dark cave, a scientist emerged with life-changing insights

children.content: Spending big money to do nothing in the dark is on the rise. Scientist Kiana Aran chronicled what happened to her when she tried it.

image.altText: headshot of Kiana wearing a black eye mask, there is a small round, black gadget on her forehead.

items.title: Why 'irresponsible' dumping of mercury caused evacuations in outback town

children.content: An outback town's hospital, service station and school were affected when students discovered vials of mercury at the tip. Experts have explained why specialists have spent days doing testing in Cunnamulla.

image.altText: two people in protective suits walk along a footpath holding a bucket and neck of an atmospheric tester

items.title: Is the government really overhauling employment services?

children.content: The government isn't rebuilding the Commonwealth Employment Service, but will its proposed changes fix a broken system?

image.altText: Rishworth speaks at the NPC lectern

items.title: Unlikely material behind Whitsundays artist's fashion collection

children.content: In the front yard of her home in the picturesque Whitsundays, Felicity Chapman strips the last pieces of flesh and sinew from a strip of barramundi skin to make wearable leather art.

gallery.altText: A woman holds out a colourful overskirt with feathers and fish scales.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds out a colourful overskirt with feathers and fish scales.

avatar.alt: A lady looks at the camera, smiling.
